implemented_slice: Added GET /v1/scoring/active-context so clients can compare refresh_token and refresh_generation before reusing graph, score, model or module state.
Activation advances active_analysis_contexts.refresh_token and refresh_generation atomically with the active scoring profile switch.
Activation responses include cache_invalidation.previous_refresh_token, cache_invalidation.refresh_token and affected modules.
Production graph/path context and home model_status now expose active_analysis_context for server-side refresh semantics.
Integration tests assert old refresh tokens are stale and matching refresh tokens are current.
Frontend model-center hydration reads /v1/scoring/active-context and maps server profile/data/score context into visible workspace module state.
Online draft profile creation through POST /v1/scoring/profiles returns the unchanged active analysis context and keeps the draft inactive until explicit transactional activation.
Frontend stale-client refresh checks refetch /v1/scoring/active-context with the previous refresh token and surface stale_client_refetched semantics.
Frontend rollback now calls POST /v1/scoring/profiles/{profileVersionId}/rollback instead of reusing the activation endpoint.
Frontend score recompute now calls POST /v1/scoring/recompute with the current active profile and refresh token before enqueueing a background job.
Score recompute enqueue returns the current active context and rejects stale refresh tokens with a 409 conflict, preserving atomic refresh semantics.

The score_recompute worker handler advances active_analysis_contexts.refresh_token after writing a new scoring_run and score_results for relationship_fact_candidate, relationship, entity, event, industry and source_document object families, so clients with the enqueue-time refresh token become stale after worker completion.
Data snapshot refresh enqueue returns the current active context and rejects stale refresh tokens with a 409 conflict, preserving atomic refresh semantics.

The data_snapshot_refresh worker handler advances active_analysis_contexts.refresh_token after writing a new active data_snapshot and data.snapshot.activated outbox event, so clients with the enqueue-time refresh token become stale after worker completion.
Transactional outbox events are written in the same transaction for model activation, score recompute request and score snapshot activation, then dispatched through the scheduler outbox path.
Live Playwright coverage now configures eei.modelApiBaseUrl.v1 and exercises activation, stale refresh and rollback against FastAPI/PostgreSQL.
Added apps.worker.app.main model_refresh_wake_contract metadata for score_recompute and data_snapshot_refresh supervisor filters.
Supervisor CLI summaries for model refresh job/event filters now bind A204/A205/A206/A209 and explicitly state that A209 4h/24h soak remains non-closed.

PostgreSQL integration coverage now executes score_recompute and data_snapshot_refresh through python -m apps.worker.app.main supervise, proving supervised wake can advance refresh tokens and leave outbox dispatch available without treating soak as complete.

A209 4h/24h soak remains a separate background production-stability gate and is not completed by this worker wake evidence.
Formal source/legal/brand clearance and production relationship approval remain outside T1303.
